pages.raw_text:
Spokanes first tuberculosis ex-
hibit and institute will open at 10
O'CLOCK tomorrow at the armory and
continue until Friday evening. The
institute is given under the auspices
of the state board of health, and all
arrangements in the way of the
slraking programs, were concluded
last tight by DR.. Wilson Johnston,
president of the state board.


The exhibits will be in charge of
J. p. tembs and lectures will be
given every afternoon at O'Clock
by local doctors, with illustrations
by a stereopticon. The speaking
program will be held at night, be-
ginning with the labor organiza-
tons Monday night.


The children of the public schools
will visit the exhibit on Tuesday,
Wednesday and Friday afternoons
and the children of the parochial
schools on Thursday afternoon. The
admission TO the institute is free.
Following is the speaking pro-
gram arranged:
